titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to Clara Davidson, 21 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from James Davidson (1877-1916) to his mother, Clara Mary (b.1852). James writes that the offensive has yet to start but doubts that it can be held off for much longer. He notes that the postal service has been disrupted by the bad weather conditions. The Somme bombardment would start three days later on 24 June.This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
21/6/16 Just a tiny line dearest
Mother â nothing has
happened as yet & I am
very well but we are
all going 16 hours
& I think the great day
cannot be far away
now so dont be
at all surprised or
worried if you hear
no more news for a
week or even more.
I daresay the postal
arrangements are
a bit upset at present.
Weather has at last
shown some signs
of improving â today
it was reasonably
warm â I do so hope
it will continue.
Have just received your
letter of 15th â thanks
so much. Am glad
to hear that you are
all well. One of
my Corpls. has just returned
from leave & was so
delighted with his visit
to the Works. I expect
he gave Father all our
news. Goodnight
Mother dear. My fondest
love to all Your loving son Jim
Back of envelope with date 26/June/16
On Active Service Written 21st. Rcd 26th
James Mrs. S. C. Davidson Seacourt Bangor Co. Down. Ireland 21-6-16
Jm Davidson
